questions: Search for the "UNIX / Linux Tutorial for Beginners" originally authored by M. Stonebank at the University of Surrey. Identify the specific version of the Creative Commons license that governs this tutorial. Based on the legal deed for that specific license version, detail the two primary 'freedoms' granted to the user (what they are free to do) and the three specific 'terms' or conditions that must be satisfied to exercise those freedoms.

rounds.question: Search for the "UNIX / Linux Tutorial for Beginners" originally authored by M. Stonebank at the University of Surrey. Identify the specific version of the Creative Commons license that governs this tutorial. Based on the legal deed for that specific license version, detail the two primary 'freedoms' granted to the user (what they are free to do) and the three specific 'terms' or conditions that must be satisfied to exercise those freedoms.
checklist_width: Freedom 1: Share (copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format)
Freedom 2: Adapt (remix, transform, and build upon the material)
Term 1: Attribution (give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, indicate if changes were made)
Term 2: NonCommercial (may not use the material for commercial purposes)
Term 3: ShareAlike (must distribute contributions under the same license as the original)
checklist_depth: License Identification: Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0)
Logic Proof: The tutorial specifically uses the 2.0 version (often indicated by a link to /by-nc-sa/2.0/), distinct from newer 4.0 recommendations.
rounds.rationale: The question is Deep because it requires linking a generic resource title ('UNIX Tutorial') via a specific author (Stonebank) to a specific, older legal framework (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0) that is not immediately obvious without visiting the source. It is Wide because the agent must then retrieve and categorize five distinct clauses (2 freedoms, 3 terms) from the license deed text.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Notice This is an older version of this license. Compared to previous versions, the 4.0 versions of all CC licenses are more user-friendly and more internationally robust . If
rounds.answer_a:
<minimax:tool_call>

rounds.answer_b:
M. Stonebank’s “UNIX / Linux Tutorial for Beginners” from the University of Surrey is governed by the Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 UK: England & Wales (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 UK) license [1]. Its legal deed grants two primary freedoms to users. First, users are free to copy, distribute, display, perform the original work, and create derivative works that transform or adapt the tutorial’s content for personal or non-commercial use [2]. Second, users are free to share the original tutorial or their derivative works with others via any medium, as long as they adhere to the license’s binding terms [2]. Three specific terms must be satisfied to exercise these freedoms. First, attribution: users must give explicit credit to M. Stonebank and the University of Surrey, include a clear license notice, and note any modifications made (without misleading others about the work’s origin) [2]. Second, non-commercial use: no original or derivative work may be used for financial gain or commercial exploitation of any kind [2]. Third, sharealike: any derivative work must be licensed under the same CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 UK license or a legally compatible equivalent, ensuring shared works maintain matching access constraints [2]. This framework balances user flexibility with protections for the tutorial’s non-commercial, community-focused intent tied to its academic origin.

## References
[1] University of Surrey. “UNIX / Linux Tutorial for Beginners by M. Stonebank.” Archived Student Tutorial Repository, https://www.surrey.ac.uk/computing/students/tutorials/unix-linux.
[2] Creative Commons. “Legal Deed: Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 UK: England & Wales.” Creative Commons License Directory, https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/uk/legalcode.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A failed to generate a final response, displaying only a raw tool call. Agent B provided a complete, accurate, and well-cited answer that correctly identified the specific license version (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 UK) and detailed the required freedoms and terms.
